# boost-core

> Framework-agnostic AI agent configuration sync for any PHP project. Author
> skills, guidelines, and slash-commands once under `.ai/`; `boost sync` fans
> them out to up to nine agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Codex, Gemini,
> Junie, Kiro, OpenCode, Amp). No framework dependency. `boost-core` is the sync
> engine; users normally install a thin **family wrapper** that bundles it with a
> curated skill set for what they are building.

Key facts for agents:

- `boost-core` is a plain Composer **library** with a `vendor/bin/boost` CLI. It
  runs no install-time code: sync is explicit (`vendor/bin/boost sync`) or opt-in
  via a `BoostAutoSync` Composer hook.
- Config lives at `boost.php` (repo root) or `.config/boost.php`, built with
  `BoostConfig::configure()->withAgents([...])` and optional `withTags()`,
  `withAllowedVendors()`, `withRemoteSkills()`, `withConventions()`.
- Skills come from three places: host `.ai/skills/`, allowlisted Composer vendor
  packages, and `withRemoteSkills()` GitHub sources. Tag filtering scopes vendor
  and remote skills per project. `metadata.boost-requires` declares hard skill
  dependencies: whenever a skill ships, its requires co-ship (tag-dropped deps
  are rescued; `withExcludedSkills()` still wins).
- Guidance files (`CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `GEMINI.md`, Copilot) are markerless,
  boost-owned, regenerated on every sync, but kept tracked. Skill and command
  directories are gitignored. A hand-edited file is never blanked.
- Coexists with [`laravel/boost`](https://github.com/laravel/boost) in Laravel
  projects via the `project-boost-laravel` wrapper (laravel/boost owns the MCP
  server and Laravel docs; the wrapper owns agent-file fan-out).

Links are absolute so they resolve when this file is fetched outside the repo;
the install guide is linked as raw Markdown for direct agent fetching.

## Installing (agents start here)

- [llms-install.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sandermuller/boost-core/main/llms-install.md):
  Executable, step-by-step install guide — detect the repo type, pick the right
  family member, install, configure agents and tags, sync, and verify. **Read
  this when asked to install or set up boost.**

## Docs

- [README.md](https://github.com/sandermuller/boost-core/blob/main/README.md):
  Full documentation — how sync works, skill sources, tag filtering, commands,
  skill rendering, autosync, file ownership, the complete CLI reference, and
  environment variables.
- [PUBLIC_API.md](https://github.com/sandermuller/boost-core/blob/main/PUBLIC_API.md):
  The frozen SemVer surface — config authoring API, CLI commands/options/
  exit-codes, Composer hooks, and plugin contracts.
- [docs/file-ownership.md](https://github.com/sandermuller/boost-core/blob/main/docs/file-ownership.md):
  The sync manifest, lifecycle reaping, the empty-assembly guard, `.config/`
  layout, and managed `.gitignore`.
- [docs/conventions.md](https://github.com/sandermuller/boost-core/blob/main/docs/conventions.md):
  Project Conventions — JSONSchema slot fill-in that injects project-specific
  context into vendor skills.
- [UPGRADING.md](https://github.com/sandermuller/boost-core/blob/main/UPGRADING.md):
  Breaking-change migrations between versions.
- [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/sandermuller/boost-core/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md):
  Full release history.

## Family wrappers

- [project-boost-laravel](https://github.com/sandermuller/project-boost-laravel):
  Laravel applications — `laravel/boost` MCP coexistence + nine-agent fan-out.
- [project-boost-php](https://github.com/sandermuller/project-boost-php): PHP
  applications on any framework or none.
- [package-boost-php](https://github.com/sandermuller/package-boost-php):
  Framework-agnostic Composer packages — package-author skills + `lean` /
  `gitattributes` commands.
- [package-boost-laravel](https://github.com/sandermuller/package-boost-laravel):
  Laravel packages — Testbench skills + `McpJsonEmitter`.
- [boost-skills](https://github.com/sandermuller/boost-skills): An example
  Composer-distributed skill catalog consumed via the vendor allowlist.
